name: next-up
title: Next Up
description: A simple all-in-one scheduler and quick glance to manage custom weekly schedules for class and more!
author: adrianbonpin

commands.name: what-s-next
commands.title: Today
commands.description: See what's coming up next in your schedule today. Shows the next class and all of today's courses with quick access to meeting links.

commands.name: schedule-for-the-week
commands.title: Schedule for the Week
commands.description: View your entire week at a glance. All courses organized by day with today's schedule highlighted.

commands.name: manage-schedules
commands.title: Manage Schedules
commands.description: Create, edit, and organize your class schedules. Add courses with multiple time slots, manage schedule groups, and set up quick-access links.

commands.name: menu-bar
commands.title: Next up Menu Bar
commands.description: Keep your next class visible in the menu bar at all times. Click to see today's full schedule and join meetings instantly.

commands.name: add-ephemeral-course
commands.title: Add Ephemeral Course
commands.description: Quickly add a temporary course for today. Automatically disappears tomorrow.

preferences.name: exportPath
preferences.title: Export Folder
preferences.description: Folder where exported schedule JSON files will be saved (e.g. ~/Downloads)

preferences.name: importFilePath
preferences.title: Import File Path
preferences.description: Full path to a previously exported next-up JSON file to import via 'Import from File' action (⌘⇧I) in Manage Schedules

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
